prompt_text: Open source or affordable AI tooling tools instead of Portkey
response:
# Open Source & Affordable AI Tooling Alternatives to Portkey

## **Open Source Options**

### **LiteLLM** (Most Popular)
- Unified API for 100+ LLM providers
- Drop-in replacement for OpenAI SDK
- Local proxy server, cost tracking, caching
- **Free & MIT licensed**
- https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm

### **LangChain / LangSmith**
- LangChain: Open source framework
- LangSmith: Paid observability (but generous free tier)
- Great for building LLM apps
- https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain

### **Ollama**
- Run LLMs locally (no API costs)
- Supports Llama, Mistral, etc.
- https://ollama.ai

### **VLLM**
- High-throughput LLM serving
- Self-hosted inference
- https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm

---

## **Affordable/Freemium Paid Options**

| Tool | Free Tier | Use Case |
|------|-----------|----------|
| **Unify.ai** | $5 credit | Multi-provider routing |
| **Modal** | Generous free tier | Serverless AI inference |
| **Hugging Face Inference API** | Free tier available | Model hosting & inference |
| **Replicate** | Pay-per-use | Model deployment |
| **Together AI** | Free tier + cheap pricing | Open source models |

---

## **DIY Stack** (Most Cost-Effective)
```
LiteLLM (routing) + OpenObserve (logs) + 
Redis (caching) + Self-hosted monitoring
```

**Best for:** Teams wanting full control & minimal vendor lock-in

---

Which use case matters most to you? (Monitoring, routing, cost control, etc.)
